_description: Every rule in this file was learned from an actual generation failure. These are NOT theoretical.

pipeline_rules.failure: Generated Frame A without script, had to regenerate when script required product in hand
pipeline_rules.enforcement: Never open image gen tool before script.json exists for the campaign

pipeline_rules.rule: Stage the pipeline. One layer at a time
pipeline_rules.failure: Combined character + expression + environment + product in one prompt. When image looked wrong, couldn't diagnose which element failed
pipeline_rules.enforcement: Character cast (neutral) → Scene test (no product) → Product integration → Frame B

pipeline_rules.rule: Character cast uses neutral expression ONLY
pipeline_rules.failure: Generated character in reaction scene. Model prioritized matching the emotion over creating a distinctive face
pipeline_rules.enforcement: Template 1 has no mood/reaction fields. Only subject and camera

prompting_rules.rule: Always use JSON prompt structure, never flat paragraphs
prompting_rules.failure: Paragraph prompts produced inconsistent, less precise results
prompting_rules.enforcement: All templates are JSON. No paragraph prompt templates exist

prompting_rules.rule: Expression intensity at 30% of what you think you need
prompting_rules.failure: Full-intensity surprise prompt produced theatrical, stock-photo-like expressions
prompting_rules.enforcement: mood.facial_expression must specify mm of mouth opening, exact eyebrow movement, and SECOND of emotion (0.3 = subtle, 2.0 = performative)

prompting_rules.rule: Gaze 2-3 degrees off-camera for selfie authenticity
prompting_rules.failure: Dead-center lens contact felt posed and AI-like
prompting_rules.enforcement: All selfie templates include 'looking slightly off-camera at phone screen' in head_and_gaze

prompting_rules.rule: Signature details on every character (2-3 unique identifiers)
prompting_rules.failure: Generic descriptions produced different-looking people each generation. No way to verify character lock
prompting_rules.enforcement: Character cast template requires primary, secondary, tertiary signature details with tier classification

product_rules.rule: Products from reference images, never from text descriptions
product_rules.failure: Text-described supplement bottle was generic, wrong colors, wrong label. Immediately reads as AI
product_rules.enforcement: Template 3 requires PRODUCT_REFERENCE image. No text-only product generation

product_rules.rule: Product scale must be specified with physical dimensions
product_rules.failure: Model guessed product size. Got lucky once but will be wrong for different product types
product_rules.enforcement: product_physical block required with height, width, material, scale_reference

product_rules.rule: Product scale must be consistent between Frame A and Frame B
product_rules.failure: Frame B (subject leaned closer) had product SMALLER than Frame A. Physically impossible
product_rules.enforcement: Frame B template includes product_scale_consistency block with physics rule

reference_image_rules.rule: Frame B needs ONLY Frame A as reference. One image, not two
reference_image_rules.failure: Sending Frame A + separate product image was redundant (product already in Frame A) and risked model confusion
reference_image_rules.enforcement: Frame B template specifies single image with combined role

reference_image_rules.rule: Phone NOT visible in frame for front-camera selfies
reference_image_rules.failure: Model rendered phone visible (mirror selfie style), adding unnecessary complexity and potential artifacts
reference_image_rules.enforcement: All selfie templates: 'phone below frame, NOT visible' + 'phone visible in frame' in negative prompt
